forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KXTFTPY5BCXGN9MFXFFSJQHA
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9280'
+title: 'Auto-compaction opt-in: makeSession(budget:compactionPrompt:) + retry-once'
+---
+THE harness-collapse centerpiece (harness plan §5, absorbed): the session that manages its own window. makeSession gains optional budget: TokenBudget and compactionPrompt: CompactionPrompt; when budget is set the session checks measured fill >= budget.trigger at turn start (ALSO DEPENDS ON ^1peq9n7 TokenBudget/contextFill — add when tooling permits multi-ref) and folds via its own compact() (swap internal, id stable, CompactionSegment checkpoint); a turn failing with exceededContextWindowSize (or the mid-turn budget error) compacts with a lowered target and retries ONCE before surfacing (caveat: retry re-runs tool calls; recording keeps both attempts). Summarizer preference flash > own model > deterministic pipeline. Explicit compact() remains for manual /compact binding upstairs.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,4 +1,25 @@
 ---
+comments:
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kya287hh57r24xyswk2smfgq
+  text: |-
+    Implementation landed:
+
+    - `RoutedModel.makeSession(instructions:workingDirectory:tools:budget:compactionPrompt:)` (RoutedLLM.swift) gains optional `budget: TokenBudget?` (default nil = opt-out) and `compactionPrompt: CompactionPrompt` (default `.default`), threaded through the internal shared builder into `makeRoutedSessionActor`.
+    - `RoutedSessionActor` gains `autoCompactionBudget`/`autoCompactionPrompt` stored properties, threaded through init, the `makeRoutedSessionActor` free function, and `fork(workingDirectory:)` (a fork inherits its parent's opt-in and fill state).
+    - `compact(prompt:budget:)` refactored: gate-acquire wrapper now delegates to a new private `fold(prompt:budget:summarizer:)` containing the actual mechanics (unchanged behavior — always summarizes via own model).
+    - New private `performAutoCompaction(prompt:budget:)`: 3-tier summarizer cascade for auto-compaction only — profile.flash's own model first (skipped if this session already is the flash slot), falling back to the session's own model on failure, falling back to the deterministic-only pipeline (`summarizer: nil`, which cannot throw) if that also fails. Calls `fold` directly (no gate acquisition — already inside `runTurn`'s gate).
+    - `runTurn` now checks `contextFill >= budget.trigger` before each turn (proactive fold, emits `.compaction(_:)` via `onEvent`) and delegates to new `runTurnAttempt(...allowOverflowRetry:...)`, which retries **once** on `LanguageModelError.contextSizeExceeded` when a budget is set: records the failed attempt exactly as before (unchanged), folds harder (target halved, floored at 0.1) via `performAutoCompaction`, emits `.compaction(_:)`, then recurses with `allowOverflowRetry: false` and empty `pendingEvents` (already re-queued onto outbox by the failed attempt's own finish) — guaranteeing at most one retry.
+    - `SessionEvent.swift` doc updated: `.compaction(_:)` is now actually emitted (was previously documented as reserved/never emitted, explicitly deferred to this task).
+
+    Tests: new `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/AutoCompactionTests.swift`, 6 tests — proactive fold preferring flash summarizer, fallback to own model when flash throws, opt-out (no budget) never auto-compacts, fork inherits budget + fires on its first turn from inherited fill, reactive retry-once success (with a real backend swap verified via replace-spy), reactive retry-once exhaustion (surfaces after exactly one failed retry, backend called exactly twice).
+
+    Note: tests were written after the implementation rather than strictly TDD-first, because this task required cross-cutting architectural research (serial-gate reentrancy, summarizer cascade design, retry/pendingEvents interaction) before a test-first API shape was obvious. Logged here per really-done's justification-required-if-deviating policy.
+
+    Verification: `swift build` clean, zero warnings. Full suite: 527 tests passing (504+18+5 across the three targets), up from the 521 baseline — zero failures. Adversarial double-check agent dispatched to review the diff before handoff.
+
+    Left in `doing` per /implement contract — not moved to review.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-24T12:40:22.833682+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KXTFTPY5BCXGN9MFXFFSJQHA
 - 01KXTFS4FNT1P5F889D1PEQ9N7

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -20,6 +20,22 @@

     Left in `doing` per /implement contract — not moved to review.
   timestamp: 2026-07-24T12:40:22.833682+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kya2rxs59j6esbhwkp9mtdve
+  text: |-
+    Adversarial double-check round 1 (dispatched via double-check agent) found one real gap: `RoutedModel.makeGuidedSession(grammar:instructions:workingDirectory:)` in GuidedGeneration.swift never forwarded `budget`/`compactionPrompt` to the shared internal builder, so guided sessions could never opt into auto-compaction even though the builder's own doc comment claims to serve "both plain and guided session surfaces." It also flagged (non-blocking) that `SessionTreeRestoration.swift`'s `restoreSessionTree` never carries the opt-in forward into a restored session tree.
+
+    Fixed:
+    - `makeGuidedSession` now takes `budget: TokenBudget? = nil, compactionPrompt: CompactionPrompt = .default` and forwards them through the shared builder — defaults preserve prior behavior exactly for existing callers.
+    - `restoreSessionTree`'s doc comment now explains restored nodes always get `nil`/`.default` (no persisted sidecar field to rehydrate from) and how to re-opt-in (manual `compact()` or a fresh fork). Doc-only change, no functional lines touched.
+    - Added `GuidedGenerationTests.guidedSessionForwardsAutoCompactionBudget` — downcasts the vended session to `RoutedSessionActor` and asserts `autoCompactionBudget`/`autoCompactionPrompt` actually landed, closing the regression-coverage gap the second double-check pass called out as a non-blocking suggestion.
+
+    Round 2 double-check verdict: PASS (traced the full forwarding chain makeGuidedSession → makeSession(grammar:...) → makeRoutedSessionActor; confirmed no other silent call-site regression; confirmed the restoration diff is doc-only).
+
+    Final verification: `swift build` clean, zero warnings. Full suite: 528 tests passing (505+18+5), zero failures.
+
+    Task left in `doing` per /implement contract, ready for /review.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-24T12:49:29.893982+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KXTFTPY5BCXGN9MFXFFSJQHA
 - 01KXTFS4FNT1P5F889D1PEQ9N7

+THE harness-collapse centerpiece (harness plan §5, absorbed): the session that manages its own window. makeSession gains optional budget: TokenBudget and compactionPrompt: CompactionPrompt; when budget is set the session checks measured fill >= budget.trigger at turn start (ALSO DEPENDS ON ^1peq9n7 TokenBudget/contextFill — add when tooling permits multi-ref) and folds via its own compact() (swap internal, id stable, CompactionSegment checkpoint); a turn failing with exceededContextWindowSize (or the mid-turn budget error) compacts with a lowered target and retries ONCE before surfacing (caveat: retry re-runs tool calls; recording keeps both attempts). Summarizer preference flash > own model > deterministic pipeline. Explicit compact() remains for manual /compact binding upstairs.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-07-24 07:52)
+
+- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift:341` — The docstring for streamEvents(to:maxTokens:) specifies the event emission order as: textDelta, then (once diff runs) toolCall/toolStatus/reasoningDelta, then turnEnded, and states that "SessionEvent/compaction(_:) is emitted before those". However, the reactive auto-compaction path in runTurnAttempt emits the compaction event AFTER the failed attempt's turnEnded event, contradicting the documented order. Callers using streamEvents in the overflow-recovery scenario will observe compaction events at a different point in the sequence than the documentation describes. Clarify the docstring to accurately describe both proactive and reactive compaction event orderings. For example: 'Compaction events may be emitted before the turn\'s other events (proactive, before generation) or after the first attempt\'s turnEnded (reactive, after context overflow), depending on whether the session auto-compacts proactively or in recovery.'.
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/GuidedGenerationTests.swift:282` — The test guidedSessionForwardsAutoCompactionBudget verifies that makeGuidedSession forwards budget/compactionPrompt parameters to the created session by inspecting the actor's fields. However, the test does not exercise the actual functionality those parameters enable — auto-compaction in a guided session. The capability was added to makeGuidedSession (ability to pass budget) but the consuming behavior (the session actually triggering auto-compaction) is never tested. AutoCompactionTests comprehensively test auto-compaction, but only for unguided sessions created via makeSession; no test exercises auto-compaction with a guided session. Add a test that creates a guided session with a budget (e.g., profile.standard.makeGuidedSession(grammar: .jsonSchema(...), budget: fixedBudget)), drives turns until contextFill reaches the trigger, and verifies that auto-compaction is emitted in the event stream. This can reuse the helper structure from AutoCompactionTests (makeTriggeredSession or similar) with makeGuidedSession as the session factory.
\ No newline at end of file

-
-## Review Findings (2026-07-24 07:52)

-- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift:341` — The docstring for streamEvents(to:maxTokens:) specifies the event emission order as: textDelta, then (once diff runs) toolCall/toolStatus/reasoningDelta, then turnEnded, and states that "SessionEvent/compaction(_:) is emitted before those". However, the reactive auto-compaction path in runTurnAttempt emits the compaction event AFTER the failed attempt's turnEnded event, contradicting the documented order. Callers using streamEvents in the overflow-recovery scenario will observe compaction events at a different point in the sequence than the documentation describes. +  id: 01kya3qjgzgtah8ejfvm130dfr
+  text: |-
+    Addressed both review findings from the 2026-07-24 07:52 pass:
+
+    1. `RoutedSession.swift` streamEvents(to:maxTokens:) docstring: fixed to describe both compaction event orderings — proactive fold emits .compaction before the turn's textDelta/toolCall/toolStatus/turnEnded; reactive fold (overflow retry) emits .compaction after the failed attempt's own turnEnded, since runTurnAttempt's catch branch calls finishTurnAndRequeueIfUnattached (which emits turnEnded) before performAutoCompaction/onEvent?(.compaction(result)).
+
+    2. Added new test `GuidedGenerationTests.guidedSessionAutoCompactsAtTrigger` (existing `guidedSessionForwardsAutoCompactionBudget` left untouched) — drives a real guided session (profile.standard.makeGuidedSession(grammar:budget:)) through 6 warm-up turns via the guided respond(to:) path with escalating usageIncrement, crossing the 0.8 trigger (contextFill == 0.9), then observes via streamEvents that the very next turn's first event is .compaction with Summarization applied and flash's summary — proving auto-compaction actually fires end-to-end for a guided session, not just that the config fields got set. Mirrors AutoCompactionTests' proven trigger-driving mechanism (own per-slot loader/container/budget-sizing helpers, documented as mirroring that file).
+
+    Verification: swift build clean, swift build --build-tests clean, zero warnings in source. Full suite: 529 tests passing (506+18+5), up from 528 baseline (+1 new test), zero failures.
+
+    Adversarial double-check agent dispatched to verify both fixes before handoff.
+
+    Task left in doing, ready for /review.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -62,5 +62,5 @@

 ## Review Findings (2026-07-24 07:52)

-- [ ] `Sources/FoundationModelsRouter/Session/RoutedSession.swift:341` — The docstring for streamEvents(to:maxTokens:) specifies the event emission order as: textDelta, then (once diff runs) toolCall/toolStatus/reasoningDelta, then turnEnded, and states that "SessionEvent/compaction(_:) is emitted before those". However, the reactive auto-compaction path in runTurnAttempt emits the compaction event AFTER the failed attempt's turnEnded event, contradicting the documented order. Callers using streamEvents in the overflow-recovery scenario will observe compaction events at a different point in the sequence than the documentation describes. Clarify the docstring to accurately describe both proactive and reactive compaction event orderings. For example: 'Compaction events may be emitted before the turn\'s other events (proactive, before generation) or after the first attempt\'s turnEnded (reactive, after context overflow), depending on whether the session auto-compacts proactively or in recovery.'.
-- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsRouterTests/GuidedGenerationTests.swift:282` — The test guidedSessionForwardsAutoCompactionBudget verifies that makeGuidedSession forwards budget/compactionPrompt parameters to the created session by inspecting the actor's fields. However, the test does not exercise the actual functionality those parameters enable — auto-compaction in a guided session. The capability was added to makeGuidedSession (ability to pass budget) but the consuming behavior (the session actually triggering auto-compaction) is never tested. AutoCompactionTests comprehensively test auto-compaction, but only for unguided sessions created via makeSession; no test exercises auto-compaction with a guided session. Add a test that creates a guided session with a budget (e.g., profile.standard.makeGuidedSession(grammar: .jsonSchema(...), budget: fixedBudget)), drives turns until contextFill reaches the trigger, and verifies that auto-compaction is emitted in the event stream. This can reuse the helper structure from AutoCompactionTests (makeTriggeredSession or similar) with makeGuidedSession as the session factory.
\ No newline at end of file
